description: Evaluation suite for the City of Yellowknife Building By-law No. 5058 skill.

tests.scenario: A client is designing a three-unit rowhouse in Yellowknife. Do they need a sprinkler system?
expected_answer_elements: Yes — sprinkler required
§61 — multi-family dwellings with three units or more must have a sprinkler system
Must comply with NFPA 13 per §61(2)
AHJ disclaimer — confirm with City of Yellowknife Building Services Division

tests.scenario: Can I build a Part 9 house with a continuous concrete foundation pinned to bedrock without engaging a structural engineer?
expected_answer_elements: Yes, under §54 — without requiring an RDP if the prescriptive requirements are met
20 mm dowels at max 1200 mm o/c (or 15 mm at 900 mm o/c), grouted min 300 mm into bedrock
Maximum wall height 3.66 m
Recommend confirming with City of Yellowknife Building Services Division

tests.scenario: A Part 9 cabin needs concrete piers on bedrock. Does Schedule B really let me avoid an RDP?
expected_answer_elements: §56 — pier foundation pinned to bedrock for a Part 9 building may be approved without an RDP if designed per Schedule B
Four pre-designed cases (Cases 1-4 at 8/10/15/20 ft spacing)
Site must match a case's building dimensions, axial/lateral loads, and height
If the project doesn't fit a Schedule B case exactly, §58 kicks in — RDP + NBC Part 4

tests.scenario: What R-value do I need for above-grade walls on a Part 9 residential build in Yellowknife using the prescriptive path?
expected_answer_elements: §64(4) Table 1 — above-grade walls Effective RSI 5.28 (R-30)
FDWR 15% max
Tighter than NBC 9.36 default; paramountcy §15 says By-law wins

tests.scenario: My building permit was issued 22 months ago and I still haven't finished. What's my situation?
expected_answer_elements: §22(6) — permits expire after 2 years (you have ~2 months)
§22(7) — may request written extension for adverse weather / strikes / material shortages / similar hardship
If only foundation built and permit lapses, §22(5) — 60 days to restore site

tests.scenario: I was issued a Stop-Work Order yesterday. Can I contest it?
expected_answer_elements: §44 — Council review on written request
14-day filing window; hearing within 21 days
§44(3) — Council decision cannot contravene the NBC
§44(5) — imminent danger override — Council/City Manager can shorten timelines

tests.scenario: What's the minimum exit width for a Group A-2 occupancy in Yellowknife?
expected_answer_elements: §4 adopts NBC 2020 in entirety — exit width governed by NBC, not this by-law
Recommend the canbc skill for authoritative NBC text
Flag §15 paramountcy — if any Yellowknife overlay exceeds NBC, the by-law applies

tests.scenario: A homeowner wants to build an 800 mm retaining wall that doesn't support anything. Permit required?
expected_answer_elements: §68 — >600 mm and <1200 mm not supporting structure → drawings per Schedule E
Schedule E provides three typologies: Reinforced Concrete / P.T. 6×6 Timber / Allan Block
Yes, a permit is required

tests.scenario: Does the applicant really need to provide a Real Property Report before occupancy?
expected_answer_elements: §29(1)(i) — yes, an up-to-date RPR to Building Services Division prior to occupancy being issued
Tied to §24 OC issuance criteria

tests.scenario: What's the voluntary fine for building without a permit?
expected_answer_elements: Schedule A(3) — $200 (individual) / $1,000 (corporation) voluntary fine
§51 — permit fees may also be doubled
Summary Conviction (§45) can reach $2,000 individual / $10,000 corporation / 6 months imprisonment
